title: benthamia drummondii
intro: Benthamia drummondii, also known as Drummond's benthamia or snake flower, is a plant species native to Western Australia. It is a flowering plant with a range of medicinal properties, particularly in its leaves, which have been traditionally used to treat various health conditions. The plant is valued for its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties, which can help to soothe wounds, reduce fever, and combat infections. Herbal preparations of Benthamia drummondii include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used topically or taken internally to harness its medicinal benefits.
